summary: Partially decompiled the main menu OnEnter routine mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter in src/melee/mn/mn_2295.c, raising its reported match from 0.00% to 99.20%. The PR replaced the function stub with C that initializes menu state, conditionally loads MnMaAll archive symbols, loads SIS menu/toy data, creates lights/fog/camera/background/panel objects, dispatches special event/100-man paths, and starts audio/state setup. It also renamed many anonymous main-menu asset globals to archive-derived MenMain* names, split a large .data blob into individual string labels in symbols.txt, added mnevent.h declarations, and corrected several prototypes needed by the new C.
what_changed: Replaced the placeholder `/// #mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter` with a C implementation taking `void* arg0_` and locally interpreting it as a three-byte struct with fields `x0`, `x1`, and `x2`.

Added `mn_8022DDA8_inline`, which creates the menu light object, attaches the LObj from `MenMain_lights`, initializes `mn_804A04F0.x12`, computes a light/index value with `mn_8022C010`, stores `mn_8022BFBC()` into `mn_804A04F0.x14`, and calls `mn_8022C068`.

Added a large conditional `lbArchive_LoadSymbols("MnMaAll", ...)` sequence for menu archive assets when `arg0->x2 != 0`, loading model descriptors for `MenMainBack_Top`, `MenMainPanel_Top`, `MenMainConTop_Top`, cursors, rules/event/IS/SS connector assets, plus `MenMain_cam`, `MenMain_lights`, and `MenMain_fog`.
Added language-sensitive SIS data loading: `SdMenu.usd` when `lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS()` is true, otherwise `SdMenu.dat`, plus `SdToy.dat`.
Hooked the new OnEnter code into existing menu setup calls including `mn_8022BCF8`, `mn_8022BE34`, `mn_8022BEDC`, `mn_80229B2C`, `mn_80229DC0`, `mn_8022B3A0`, `lbAudioAx_80023F28`, and `lb_8001CE00`.

Handled special main-menu entry cases: case `7` calls `mnEvent_8024E838(gm_801BEB80(), 0)`, case `33` calls `mnHyaku_8024CD64(arg0->x1)`, and the default creates a process using `mn_803EB6B0[mn_804A04F0.x0].x10` or falls back to `mn_8022DB10`.

Renamed anonymous BSS model descriptors such as `mn_804A0508`, `mn_804A0518`, `mn_804A0528`, and `mn_804A0538` to asset-based names like `MenMainBack_Top`, `MenMainPanel_Top`, `MenMainConTop_Top`, and `MenMainCursor_Top`, and updated users throughout the file.
Renamed SBSS globals `mn_804D6BBC`, `mn_804D6BC0`, and `mn_804D6BC4` to `MenMain_lights`, `MenMain_fog`, and `MenMain_cam` and updated fog/camera/light loading call sites.
Extended `mn_unk1` in `mn_2295.h` with fields `x12`, `x13`, and `x14`, matching new state accesses in the decompiled OnEnter helper.

Refined prototypes in `mn_2295.h`: `mn_8022BFBC` now returns `int`, `mn_8022C068` is typed as `void mn_8022C068(HSD_LObj*, int, int)`, `fn_8022C128` is typed as `void fn_8022C128(HSD_GObj*)`, and `mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter` now takes `void*`.
Changed `gmMainLib_8015ECB0` return type from `u8` to `int` in both source and header, matching the way its result is passed to `lbAudioAx_80023F28` in the new OnEnter code.
Populated `src/melee/mn/mnevent.h` with include guards and declarations for event-menu functions, including a typed `void mnEvent_8024E838(int, int)` needed by `mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter`.
Split a former single `.data` symbol `lbl_803EBB70` of size `0xA90` into many individual string labels from `lbl_803EBB70` through `lbl_803EC5F4`, each marked `data:string` with explicit sizes.
Added `u8 data_pad[0xd5c - 0xc4c] = { 0 };` near the new OnEnter implementation, indicating a deliberate padding/layout-preservation tactic for the surrounding data/function region.
smart_moves: Used archive symbol names directly as C global names for model descriptors, camera, lights, and fog, making the decompiled code self-documenting and reducing dependence on raw address-style `mn_804A*` names.
Kept the `lbArchive_LoadSymbols` argument order explicit and grouped by asset, which is useful for matching varargs archive loading while documenting the contents of `MnMaAll`.
Introduced a small inline helper for the light-object setup, likely to preserve codegen around the OnEnter function while isolating a logically coherent setup sequence.
Corrected callee prototypes before using them from the matched code, especially for `HSD_LObj*` and `HSD_GObj*` parameters, avoiding broad `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` pollution in newly decompiled code.
Split the large string blob in `symbols.txt` into fine-grained `data:string` symbols, improving relocation/reference readability and making future matching around this menu data easier.
Preserved partially understood argument data with a local anonymous struct rather than over-naming fields without evidence.
decomp_lessons: For archive-heavy menu entry functions, first identify and name the asset globals from `lbArchive_LoadSymbols`; those names then clarify nearly every later JObj, CObj, fog, and light use.
Partial matches can still be highly valuable when they replace a large stub and expose control flow, initialization order, and asset dependencies, even if a small unmatched percentage remains.
When a function uses varargs symbol loading, matching often depends on exact symbol order and exact pointer destinations; keep the load list close to the binary order.
Menu OnEnter routines may need cross-system headers: this PR needed archive, card/language, event menu, Hyakunin/Kumite, game mode, and audio declarations to express the real setup sequence.
Do not leave all data as one anonymous blob when it is clearly strings; individual `data:string` symbols make subsequent decompilation and relocation analysis much easier.
Return-type width can affect matching and call-site codegen; `gmMainLib_8015ECB0` was widened from `u8` to `int` in source and header to support the new caller context.
When struct shape is only partly known, extending with observed fields such as `x12`, `x13`, and `x14` is preferable to inventing semantic names.
naming_conventions: Asset-backed main-menu descriptors were named with the archive/object stem, e.g. `MenMainBack_Top`, `MenMainPanel_Top`, `MenMainConTop_Top`, `MenMainCursor_Top`.

Related cursor descriptor names preserve archive suffixes and numbering: `MenMainCursorTr01_Top` through `MenMainCursorTr04_Top`, `MenMainCursorRl01_Top` through `MenMainCursorRl05_Top`, `MenMainCursorIs_Top`, and `MenMainCursorSs_Top`.
Scene-level assets use concise semantic names: `MenMain_lights`, `MenMain_fog`, and `MenMain_cam`.
Archive symbol strings follow the pattern `<AssetName>_joint`, `<AssetName>_animjoint`, `<AssetName>_matanim_joint`, and `<AssetName>_shapeanim_joint`.
Unknown structs and fields remain conservative, such as `mn_unk1`, `x12`, `x13`, `x14`, and the local argument fields `x0`, `x1`, `x2`.
Event menu declarations use the existing `mnEvent_` prefix and address-based suffixes where semantic names are not established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted a placeholder function into C while accepting a partial match, as confirmed by the bot report showing `mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter` at 99.20%.
Used an inline helper `mn_8022DDA8_inline` to shape codegen around a repeated or sensitive light setup block.
Added explicit padding data `data_pad[0xd5c - 0xc4c]` to preserve layout near the newly decompiled region.
Adjusted function prototypes and return types to reduce implicit casts and improve generated code shape.
Split one large `.data` object into many typed string symbols so references can resolve to exact labels instead of offsets inside a monolithic blob.
Renamed BSS/SBSS symbols in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` in sync with C declarations, keeping symbol map and source names aligned.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported one unmatched-function improvement: `main/melee/mn/mn_2295` function `mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter`, 2959 bytes, from 0.00% before to 99.20% after.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish the remaining 0.80% of `mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter` or remove `data_pad`.
Search for later semantic naming of `mn_804A04F0`, `mn_unk1`, and the local OnEnter argument struct fields `x0`, `x1`, and `x2`.
Check whether later PRs type the many `mnevent.h` declarations still left as `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`.
Search for additional PRs that use the `MenMain*_Top` archive symbol naming convention in other main-menu functions.
Investigate whether the `gmMainLib_8015ECB0` return type change from `u8` to `int` affected other call sites or later matching work.
